content: If a user reports that their email is not recognised or no account is linked, the account may not be activated yet.
Turas activates new starter accounts weekly using SWISS employment data. To activate an account, go to the Healthboard Administrator tab and select “View New SWISS Employment.”

Search by date range. Users awaiting activation will show with a star icon. Select the user and enter their email address exactly as shown in Outlook Contacts.

---

Two employments / SAS and NHS / access to second organisation
Some users have two employments (e.g. SAS and NHS), but Turas only supports one core organisation.
Ask the user to check which organisation is set as their core organisation in their account settings.

Access to a second organisation must be requested via learning@nes.scot.nhs.uk or the “Turas Learn Onboarding Support Team” in Teams. Include the user’s Turas email address.

---

Add staff to line manager / appraisal setup / responsibility level
Before assigning staff in Turas Appraisal, check that the line manager or reviewer has the correct responsibility level.
Search for the user, open their profile, and select “Update Employee.”

Make sure the correct responsibility level is ticked, then save before assigning staff.

---

Assign line manager / assign reviewer / single user
To assign a line manager or reviewer to an individual, use the Turas Appraisal Admin site.
Search for the employee, open “User Profile” from the Actions menu, and select “Update.”

Change the line manager or reviewer details and submit the update.

---

Assign multiple staff / bulk assign manager or reviewer
To assign multiple employees to the same line manager or reviewer, use the “Add to List” option.
Search for each employee and add them to the employment list.

Select “Employments in List” and use “Assign Line Manager” or “Assign Reviewer.” The list will clear after changes are made.

---

Merge accounts / email already in use
If you see the message “This email address is already in use,” this indicates a duplicate account and a merge is required.
Most account merges should be handled by Learning and Development Administrators.

Escalate requests involving senior staff if required.

---

Content not appearing / missing modules / Turas content issue
If a user cannot find learning content in Turas, the issue is often due to their organisation setting.
Ask the user to check their organisation is set to SAS in their profile.

They should log out and back in after updating. If the issue continues, escalate to a Learning Technologist.

---

Duplicate accounts / two employments / SWISS and manual account
Duplicate accounts can occur when one account is created manually and another is created through SWISS.
The manually created employment can be terminated using the “Update” option and selecting “Terminate Employment.”

SWISS-created employments cannot be terminated, which helps identify the correct account to keep.

---

Employee moved board / change organisation / same email
If an employee moves to a different board but keeps the same email address, their SWISS ID does not change.
No new account is needed.
Update the organisation in their user profile to reflect the correct board.

If a user reports that no account is linked to their email address or that their email address is not registered, it may be because the account has not yet been activated. Each week, Turas receives a report of new starters, and accounts can be activated through Turas Appraisal by going to the Healthboard Administrator tab and selecting “View New SWISS Employment.” After choosing the relevant date range and clicking search, all employees whose accounts are awaiting activation will appear, with a star next to their name indicating that activation is pending. Once the employee is selected, the system will prompt for an email address, which must be taken directly from Outlook Contacts to ensure accuracy.

Some users may have two employments, one with SAS and one with NHS. Since Turas accounts only support one core organisation, the user must first check which organisation is set as their core organisation within their account settings. NHS can then manually add permission for the user to access content from the second organisation. Requests of this nature can either be sent to learning@nes.scot.nhs.uk or raised through the “Turas Learn Onboarding Support Team” channel on Teams. When raising the request, ensure that the employee’s registered Turas email address is included.

When adding staff under management in Turas for appraisal purposes, it is important to first confirm that the person being assigned as the line manager or reviewer has the correct responsibility level applied to their account. This can be checked by searching for the individual, opening their user profile, selecting “Update Employee,” and confirming that the relevant responsibility level is ticked before saving the changes.

The allocation itself is completed manually through the Turas Appraisal Admin site. Individual employees can be assigned by searching for them, selecting “User Profile” from the Actions menu, choosing “Update,” and then changing the line manager and/or reviewer details before submitting. If multiple employees need to be assigned to the same person, each individual should first be added to a list using the “Add to List” option.

Once all relevant staff have been added, selecting “Employments in List” allows the administrator to use the “Assign Line Manager” or “Assign Reviewer” functions. It is important to note that the system does not retain the employment list after changes are made, meaning that if both the line manager and reviewer require updating, the search and list-building process must be repeated.

Requests to merge accounts can usually be identified when attempting to change an email address within the Turas Healthboard Administration site. If the message “This email address is already in use, please enter another or leave the field empty to create new email address” appears, a merge is required. It has been agreed with Paul Rooney that Learning and Development Administrators should manage these requests unless they relate to more senior positions.

If content cannot be found within Turas, the issue is often caused by the user’s organisation not being set to SAS within their user profile. In these cases, the user should first be asked to check and update their organisation setting if necessary, then log out and back into Turas. This usually resolves the issue and allows the missing modules to appear. If the problem continues after this step, the issue should be escalated to the Learning Technologists, such as Robina Nicholson or Dave Brand.

Duplicate accounts can sometimes occur where two employments exist for the same person. This usually happens when one account has been manually created while another has been added automatically through the SWISS system. In these situations, the manually created employment can be terminated. This is done by selecting “Update” from the Actions menu and using the “Terminate Employment” option at the bottom of the page. The SWISS-generated employment cannot be terminated, which helps identify which account should remain active.

When an employee moves boards but retains the same email address, their SWISS ID remains unchanged. In these cases, the user can simply update the board listed in their user profile to reflect the correct organisation.
